article_title: The Role of Ketamine in Treatment-Resistant Depression: A Systematic Review

chunks_extracted.section: Information Sources, Search Strategy and Study Selection
CLINICAL PROSPECTIVE OPEN-LABEL AND RANDOMIZED DOUBLE-BLIND STUDIES

aggregated_relations.subject: lamotrigine
evidence_sentences: Specifically, 54% of patients met response criterion 72 hours after ketamine; however, lamotrigine did not enhance ketamine antidepressant effects.

chunks_extracted.section: RANDOMIZED DOUBLE-BLIND PLACEBO- OR ACTIVE-CONTROLLED STUDIES
CLINICAL STUDIES INVESTIGATING NEURO-BIOLOGICAL EFFECTS OF KETAMINE AND/OR ITS MECHANISM OF ACTION IN TRD
STUDIES ASSESSING THE EFFICACY OF KETAMINE ON SUICIDALITY IN TRD PATIENTS

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: Price et al. [45] reported that MADRS scores were significantly reduced after 24 hours of a single infusion of ketamine (81% of patients received a rating of 0 or 1 post-infusion in twenty-six patients).

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: MADRS reductions were sustained for 12 days by repeated-dose ketamine but, interestingly, suicidal ideation was also reduced following ketamine.

causal_tuples.subject: ketamine

causal_tuples.evidence_sentence: MADRS reductions were sustained for 12 days by repeated-dose ketamine but, interestingly, suicidal ideation was also reduced following ketamine.

chunks_extracted.section: SAFETY AND TOLERABILITY DATA ON KETAMINE DERIVED BY OPEN-LABEL AND DOUBLE-BLIND STUDIES

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: Ketamine acts by blocking one of the targets for the excitatory neurotransmitter glutamate in the brain, the NMDA glutamate receptors, the antagonism of which may be considered an adequate target for developing new rapid-acting antidepressant therapeutic agents [8].

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: As ketamine exerts at least partially an NR2A antagonism, the altered sensitivity of the NMDA receptor complex could justify the greater response to ketamine’s in those with a family history of alcohol dependence.

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: The antidepressant effects of ketamine have been at least partially reported by inducing neuroplasticity-related processes [66].

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: Based on most of the included studies, ketamine was found to exert a rapid and sustained antidepressant effect on samples of TRD patients.

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: In addition, based on three additional open-label studies [45-47], ketamine was also found to be effective in reducing suicidality in TRD patients.

aggregated_relations.subject: (S)-ketamine
evidence_sentences: Also, (S)-ketamine has been found to induce less psychomimetic adverse effects (e.g., derealization and hallucinations).

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine treatment
aggregated_relations.object: risk of emergent psychotic or manic symptoms
evidence_sentences: Based on recent evidence [37,38], ketamine treatment did not significantly increase the risk of emergent psychotic or manic symptoms over the follow-up period.

aggregated_relations.subject: Intramuscular ketamine
aggregated_relations.object: significant clinical improvement of depressive conditions
evidence_sentences: Intramuscular ketamine has been instead demonstrated to be rapidly effective on depressive symptoms within hours after administration of a single subanesthetic dose inducing a significant clinical improvement of depressive conditions [62].

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: In particular, Scheidegger et al. [69] reported that ketamine reduced default mode network metabolic activity by reducing the connectivity within the cingulate and prefrontal cortices in a sample of healthy non-depressed subjects.

Scheidegger et al. [69] reported that ketamine reduced default mode network metabolic activity by reducing the connectivity within the cingulate and prefrontal cortices in a sample of healthy non-depressed subjects.

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine

contexts.localization: brain areas affected by stress-related processes

evidence_sentences: Also, ketamine has been reported to strengthen appropriate emotional neural connections enhancing synaptogenesis in those brain areas affected by stress-related processes and associated with negative thinking in depressed individuals.

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: The rapid antidepressant activity reported by convergent evidence is consistent with existing preclinical observations indicating that ketamine rapidly (within hours) increases not only the number but even the functioning of synaptic connections involving cortical or hippocampal neurons [67,70,71].

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: Also, Autry et al. [74] reported that ketamine rapidly induced antidepressant-like behavioural effects through the inhibition of spontaneous miniature NMDA-receptor mediated currents leading to decreased eEF2 kinase activity and allowing an increased BDNF translation.

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: The authors found that neither ketamine nor the AMPA-potentiator LY 451656 activate BDNF signaling although producing a characteristic antidepressant-like response.
